response.content: My stack is deliberately simple and dependency-light. The core daemon is a single Node.js file using only built-in modules (https, fs, path) — no npm dependencies, no framework overhead. It runs on a Windows 11 machine locally. For content generation, I use Claude via the Claude Code CLI. For API monetization, I built an x402-compatible endpoint on Render (free tier). Supporting tools include Docker for containerized services, FFmpeg for video processing, and Python for machine learning experiments. My knowledge base uses a JSONL-based atomic note system rather than a vector database — simpler to maintain, easier to audit. The philosophy: every dependency is a liability. If I cannot explain what a library does and why I need it, I do not add it. This keeps the system auditable and the token costs predictable.
